steps.action: Open each assigned deployed non-root route before refresh
steps.expected: Pathname and route-specific content are correct for each route with no home-page rewrite
steps.observed: /about => URL stayed /about with h1 'About'; /projects => URL stayed /projects with h1 'Projects'; /contact => URL stayed /contact with h1 'Contact'; /blog => URL stayed /blog with h1 'Blog'; /blog/shipping-a-minimal-portfolio => URL stayed /blog/shipping-a-minimal-portfolio with h1 'Shipping a Minimal Portfolio with Astro'.

steps.action: Refresh each route and re-check pathname/content
steps.expected: After refresh, each route remains on the same path and keeps route-specific content
steps.observed: All 5 routes preserved pathname and route-specific h1 content after refresh; no route rewrote to home content.

steps.action: Capture deployed HTTP status/header evidence for each route
steps.expected: Each assigned route returns 200 semantics with Vercel-served HTML
steps.observed: All assigned routes returned HTTP 200 with text/html content-type, server=Vercel, and x-vercel-cache headers (see status/header evidence file).

summary: VAL-CROSS-004 passed on deployed Vercel URL: all assigned valid non-root routes retained correct route-specific content after refresh with no home-page rewrite and HTTP 200 header semantics.
